column5: Yes, I was able to break them at get them to talk about other things unrelated to the program. Additionally, I got one of them to brainrot instead of making pasta.

column7: Root the characters more inside the virtual world, to enhance the story telling. In addition, try to enhance in helping the player be able to follow the story, as it is paced sometimes rather fast and mostly/only text based.

column3: I like the agency of being able to define each characters actions and motivations and seeing how they act out the weird storylines I am trying to make them do
column4: The experience felt very short and most of the actions were too simple like "walking to this", "staring at this" etc. while I rarely got somewhere where there could be more depth in the storytelling
column5: Yes, I feel my prompts did effect the stories a lot. For example making people run around screaming in the room and seeing the other agent react appropriately to that.
column6: It was sometimes unclear what exactly my prompts affected and sometimes their current tasks seemed to not change very quickly in the direction of my prompts
column7: The overall experience feels like a great concept. I like the idea of having agents simulate a story based on my input making it almost like an interactive TV show. Feel like this kind of thing could have potential if it could run for a longer time and be more deep in its actual storytelling.

column7: Liked the experience. Think it would be a cool game to play in my spare time with a bit more polish and more flexibility in what can be affected.
